llm_output: According to the San Francisco Housing Code Section 504(a)(5), showerheads must have a flow rate of no more than 3 gallons per minute.
Showerheads of the ball-joint type that cannot easily be removed from the wall without structural alteration are exempt from the 3 GPM flow rate requirement.
Every dwelling unit must be provided with a water closet, lavatory, and bathtub or shower in good working condition.
The heating system must maintain a room temperature of at least 70 degrees Fahrenheit at the midpoint of the room, measured 4 feet 6 inches above the floor.
Heating must be available for at least 11 hours between 6:00 AM and midnight.
Every habitable room must have windows with a total area of at least one-tenth of the floor area, with a minimum of 12 square feet.
Garages in apartment houses may only be used for the storage of automobiles.
Individual towels must be provided for each guest in hotels and must be laundered after each guest's use.
Cabinets installed over cooking areas must have at least 30 inches of clearance above the cooking surface.

llm_output: Buildings must be maintained free of nuisances as defined by the housing code.
Every dwelling unit must have at least one room with a minimum floor area of 120 square feet.
Natural lighting requires windows of at least one-tenth the floor area with a 12 square foot minimum.
Cabinets above cooking surfaces require at least 30 inches of clearance.
All wood used in building construction must be protected against termite damage.
Ventilation openings must be at least 1.5 square feet per 50 linear feet of exterior wall.
Apartment house garages are restricted to automobile storage only.
Heating must maintain 70°F at 4'6" above the floor for 11 hours between 6AM and midnight.
Garbage compartments must have self-closing doors and cannot use wooden platforms for flooring.
Windows and doors leading to unheated areas must be weatherstripped.
Safety devices and fire escapes must be maintained in good working condition.
All parts of the building must be kept in a clean and sanitary condition.
All bedding provided in hotels must be clean and sanitary.
Dangerous articles must not be stored in common areas of residential buildings.
A caretaker must be provided for apartment houses with 16 or more apartments.
